# Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR)
## Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript

| | |
|---|---|
| **Date** | February 10, 2026 |
| **Time** | 5:00 PM ET |
| **Participants** | Alexander Karp, CEO; Ryan Taylor, CFO; Shyam Sankar, CTO; Ann Choe, Head of Investor Relations |

---

## Operator

Good afternoon, and welcome to Palantir Technologies' Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call. All participants are in listen-only mode. A question-and-answer session will follow the formal presentation. This call is being recorded.

I would now like to turn the call over to Ann Choe, Head of Investor Relations.

---

## Ann Choe — Head of Investor Relations

Thank you, operator. Good afternoon, everyone, and thank you for joining us. Today's call will include forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied. Please refer to our SEC filings for a more detailed discussion of risk factors. We will also reference certain non-GAAP financial measures during today's call. Reconciliations to the most comparable GAAP measures are available in our earnings press release.

With that, I'll turn the call over to Alex.

---

## Alexander Karp — Chief Executive Officer

Thank you, Ann. Good afternoon, everyone.

2025 was a landmark year for Palantir. We delivered $3.12 billion in total revenue, growing 29% year-over-year, with U.S. commercial revenue accelerating to 54% growth. More importantly, we are at the center of the most significant technological transformation of our generation — the deployment of artificial intelligence in enterprise and government operations at scale.

Let me share a few numbers that illustrate our momentum. We closed 187 deals worth more than $1 million in Q4, up from 128 in the year-ago quarter. Our U.S. government business grew 23% year-over-year to $1.48 billion, driven by expanded deployments across the Department of Defense, Intelligence Community, and civilian agencies. Our commercial business reached $1.64 billion, growing 35% and now representing 53% of total revenue — a milestone that reflects the fundamental shift in our business composition.

The Artificial Intelligence Platform, or AIP, which we launched in 2023, has become the primary growth engine for our commercial business. AIP revenue reached approximately $840 million in 2025, up from $310 million in 2024. What makes AIP transformative is not just the revenue trajectory — it's the way it changes customer engagement. Customers are moving from proof-of-concept to production deployment faster than anything we have seen in our history. The median time from first AIP bootcamp to production deployment decreased from 14 weeks in Q1 2025 to 8 weeks in Q4 2025.

I want to be direct about something. The demand environment we are seeing today is unlike anything in our 22-year history. We are not capacity-constrained on the product side. We are capacity-constrained on the talent side. We need more forward-deployed engineers, more solutions architects, more people who can help our customers transform their operations. We added 340 net new employees in Q4, our largest hiring quarter ever, and we plan to add 500 or more in Q1 2026.

Let me turn it over to Ryan for the financial details.

---

## Ryan Taylor — Chief Financial Officer

Thank you, Alex.

I'll walk through our fourth quarter and full year results, followed by our guidance for 2026.

### Q4 2025 Results

Fourth quarter revenue was $845 million, up 30% year-over-year and 8% sequentially, exceeding our guidance range of $795 million to $815 million.

Breaking this down by segment:
- **U.S. Government revenue**: $395 million, up 22% year-over-year. Growth was driven by expanded deployment of Maven Smart System across combatant commands and a significant new contract with the Department of Veterans Affairs worth $145 million over five years.
- **U.S. Commercial revenue**: $278 million, up 54% year-over-year and 12% sequentially. This represents our sixth consecutive quarter of accelerating U.S. commercial growth. We added 72 net new U.S. commercial customers in Q4, bringing the total to 498.
- **International Government revenue**: $98 million, up 12% year-over-year. Growth was driven by expanded NATO partnerships and new contracts in the Indo-Pacific region.
- **International Commercial revenue**: $74 million, up 18% year-over-year. We saw particular strength in the UK, Australia, and Japan markets.

**Total contract value (TCV)** signed in Q4 was $1.28 billion, up 40% year-over-year. Remaining deal value (RDV) at year-end was $5.4 billion, up 35% from $4.0 billion at the end of 2024.

Moving to profitability — adjusted operating income in Q4 was $338 million, representing a 40% adjusted operating margin. This compares to $267 million, or 41% margin, in Q4 2024. The slight margin compression reflects our deliberate acceleration of hiring and go-to-market investment. On a GAAP basis, operating income was $142 million, or 16.8% margin.

**GAAP net income** for Q4 was $126 million, or $0.05 per diluted share. Adjusted net income was $298 million, or $0.12 per diluted share, compared to $0.09 in Q4 2024.

Free cash flow in Q4 was $287 million, representing a 34% margin. For the full year, free cash flow was $1.04 billion, up 48% year-over-year.

### Full Year 2025 Results

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | Growth |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Revenue | $3,120M | $2,418M | +29% |
| U.S. Government | $1,480M | $1,205M | +23% |
| U.S. Commercial | $952M | $618M | +54% |
| International Government | $378M | $338M | +12% |
| International Commercial | $310M | $257M | +21% |
| Adjusted Operating Income | $1,216M | $948M | +28% |
| Adjusted Operating Margin | 39.0% | 39.2% | -20 bps |
| GAAP Net Income | $462M | $312M | +48% |
| Adjusted EPS | $0.43 | $0.33 | +30% |
| Free Cash Flow | $1,040M | $702M | +48% |
| Rule of 40 Score | 68% | 65% | +3 pts |

We ended the year with $4.6 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, and zero debt. Our balance sheet is a fortress, and we intend to maintain it as such.

### 2026 Guidance

For Q1 2026, we expect:
- Revenue of $870 million to $890 million (28-31% year-over-year growth)
- Adjusted operating income of $354 million to $366 million (approximately 41% margin)

For the full year 2026, we expect:
- Revenue of $3.89 billion to $3.95 billion (25-27% year-over-year growth)
- U.S. commercial revenue growth of 45% or higher
- Adjusted operating income of $1.55 billion to $1.58 billion (approximately 40% margin)
- Adjusted free cash flow of $1.25 billion to $1.35 billion

I want to highlight that our guidance reflects a cautious approach to international government spending, where we see some near-term uncertainty related to European defense procurement timelines. Our U.S. business assumptions are underpinned by a robust pipeline and strong conversion rates.

Let me turn it to Shyam to discuss our technology platform.

---

## Shyam Sankar — Chief Technology Officer

Thanks, Ryan.

I want to focus on three technology themes that are defining our trajectory.

First, **AIP adoption**. In Q4, we conducted 186 AIP bootcamps, up from 92 in Q4 2024. But the quality of engagement is changing. We are now seeing C-suite executives personally attending bootcamps — 34% of Q4 bootcamps had a CEO, COO, or division president in the room, compared to 12% a year ago. This is not a technology evaluation anymore. This is a strategic transformation conversation.

Second, **ontology-driven AI**. Our fundamental differentiator remains the Ontology — the semantic layer that connects AI models to real-world operations. In 2025, we expanded the Ontology to support large language model integration natively, allowing customers to deploy LLM-powered workflows that are grounded in operational data and subject to governance controls. We now support integration with over 30 LLM providers, including GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral, giving customers flexibility while maintaining operational control through the Ontology.

Third, **edge AI deployment**. Our Gotham platform's edge capabilities, initially developed for military applications, are now being deployed in commercial settings — factory floors, oil rigs, hospital networks — where low-latency, air-gapped AI inference is required. Edge deployments grew 85% year-over-year and now represent approximately 15% of total deployments.

With that, let's open it up for questions.

---

## Question and Answer Session

### Dan Ives — Wedbush Securities

**Dan Ives**: Great quarter. Alex, can you talk about the competitive landscape for AIP? We're seeing Microsoft, Salesforce, and others launching enterprise AI platforms. How sustainable is your competitive moat?

**Alexander Karp**: Dan, I appreciate the question. I've heard this concern many times — in 2008 when people said Google would replace us, in 2015 when big data platforms were going to make us irrelevant, and now with enterprise AI platforms. Here's what I'll say: the companies you mention are building horizontal AI tools. We build systems that operate in the world's most complex, consequential environments. When an F-35 pilot needs real-time intelligence fusion at 40,000 feet, or when a hospital network needs to optimize 50,000 patient workflows simultaneously, you need more than a chatbot wrapper. You need an operational AI system grounded in an ontology that reflects the physical world. That is what we build, and I believe no one else is close to replicating it.

**Ryan Taylor**: I'd add the data point that our win rates in competitive evaluations have actually increased in 2025 — we won 78% of competitive deals in Q4, up from 72% in Q4 2024. When customers do rigorous evaluations, we tend to win.

---

### Brent Thill — Jefferies

**Brent Thill**: Ryan, the U.S. commercial growth is remarkable at 54%. How should we think about the sustainability of that growth rate? And can you break down how much is expansion versus new logos?

**Ryan Taylor**: Sure, Brent. Of the $952 million in U.S. commercial revenue, approximately 60% came from existing customers through expansion and upsell, and 40% from new customer cohorts acquired in the past 12 months. Our net dollar retention rate in U.S. commercial was 137% in Q4, which means existing customers are increasing spending significantly.

On sustainability — we see a long runway. We have 498 U.S. commercial customers, which represents less than 5% penetration of our identified addressable market of roughly 10,000 enterprises in the U.S. that could benefit from our platform. The average U.S. commercial customer is spending approximately $1.9 million annually, but our top cohort of customers — those who've been with us for three-plus years — averages $8.5 million. That gap represents enormous upsell opportunity.

---

### Keith Weiss — Morgan Stanley

**Keith Weiss**: Shyam, you mentioned edge AI deployments growing 85%. Can you give us a sense of the revenue contribution and margin profile of edge versus cloud deployments?

**Shyam Sankar**: Keith, edge deployments are currently about 15% of total deployments by count, contributing approximately 10% of revenue. The margin profile is actually favorable — edge deployments typically carry higher software margins because customers are deploying in environments where they value the software's operational impact enormously and there's less cloud infrastructure cost on our side. We do have higher upfront implementation costs for edge, but lifetime margins are attractive.

Looking ahead, we expect edge to become 20-25% of deployments by the end of 2026, driven by adoption in manufacturing, energy, and healthcare verticals.

---

### Mariana Perez — Bank of America

**Mariana Perez**: Alex, can you comment on the recent executive order on AI and how it might impact Palantir's government business? Also, any update on the TITAN program?

**Alexander Karp**: On the executive order — we welcome greater clarity on AI governance in government. Palantir has always operated under the most stringent security and compliance frameworks. If anything, increased AI regulation benefits us because it raises the bar that competitors must clear. We have FedRAMP High, IL-5 and IL-6 certifications, and we're working toward IL-7. Most AI companies cannot operate at these classification levels.

On TITAN — the Army's Tactical Intelligence Targeting Access Node program — we continue to be the prime contractor. The system achieved initial operating capability in Q3 2025, and we received positive feedback from the field. We expect TITAN to enter full-rate production in 2026, which we anticipate will contribute $80 million to $100 million in annual revenue at maturity.

---

### Raimo Lenschow — Barclays

**Raimo Lenschow**: Ryan, you mentioned plans to hire 500+ in Q1. That's a significant acceleration. How should we think about the impact on margins? And are you giving any signal about moving from your 40% adjusted operating margin target?

**Ryan Taylor**: Great question, Raimo. We are very deliberately leaning into growth right now. We believe this is a generational opportunity to capture market share while demand is accelerating. We will not sacrifice the long-term opportunity to optimize short-term margins.

That said, we expect adjusted operating margins to remain in the 39-41% range for 2026. We can maintain margins while accelerating hiring because revenue growth is giving us significant operating leverage on our existing infrastructure. Our revenue per employee increased 12% in 2025 to approximately $1.05 million, and we expect further improvement in 2026.

We are not changing our long-term target of 40%+ adjusted operating margins. We believe we can invest aggressively in growth while maintaining industry-leading profitability.

---

### Tyler Radke — Citi

**Tyler Radke**: Ryan, one more on the guidance — the 25-27% growth guidance for 2026 seems conservative given you just did 29% and the pipeline commentary sounds very positive. Is there something specific driving the deceleration assumption?

**Ryan Taylor**: Tyler, our guidance methodology hasn't changed — we provide ranges we have high confidence in achieving. I'd note that we've exceeded the high end of our revenue guidance for 12 consecutive quarters. The 25-27% range primarily reflects conservatism on international government timing. If international government growth reaccelerates to the mid-teens, that would represent upside to our guidance.

**Alexander Karp**: I'll just add — we don't guide to impress. We guide to deliver. Our track record speaks for itself.

---

## Operator

This concludes the question-and-answer session. I'd like to turn the call back to Alex for closing remarks.

---

## Alexander Karp — Closing Remarks

Thank you all for joining. I'll close with this thought. Twenty-two years ago, we started this company with a conviction that software could be built to solve the world's hardest problems. For most of that time, we were misunderstood — too niche, too dependent on government, too unconventional. Today, we are a $3.1 billion revenue company growing nearly 30%, profitable on both a GAAP and adjusted basis, with the strongest demand environment in our history.

The world is waking up to the reality that AI is not a chatbot. AI is a decision-making system that must be grounded in operational reality, governed by institutional values, and deployed in the most complex environments imaginable. That is exactly what we build. We will continue to invest aggressively, hire the best people, and deliver for our customers and shareholders.

Thank you, and good evening.

---

## Operator

This concludes today's conference call. You may now disconnect.
